name: bhy_byte_identical_pin
purpose: Pins benjamini_hochberg_adjust (BHY step-up) to exact output values for N=10 p-values. Any refactor of the step-up direction, running-min order, or Yekutieli c(N) computation produces a different adjusted vector and fails this test. Tolerance 1e-15 catches numerical rearrangements.
provenance: Hand-derived in Python using the BHY step-up algorithm independently of autotuner.py: sort p ascending; for rank k=N..1: candidate = (N*c(N)/k)*p_k; running_min = min(running_min, candidate); p_adj_k = clamp(running_min, 0, 1). References: Harvey & Liu 2015 DOI 10.3905/jpm.2015.42.1.013; Benjamini, Hochberg & Yekutieli 2001, Annals of Statistics 29(4).

yekutieli_c_n_pins.purpose: Pin c(N) = sum(1/j, j=1..N) for N in [1,5,10,100,500]. Catches a log-approximation substitution (c(N) approx ln(N)+gamma_em) which under-corrects at small N.

log_approx_comparison.note: c(500) = 6.7928 vs ln(500)+0.5772 = 6.7918. The ~1% gap is small but present. At N=5: c(5) = 2.2833 vs ln(5)+0.5772 = 2.1867 -- a 4.4% under-correction that the harmonic-number test catches.
